Arteris introduces FlexGen, a Smart Network-on-Chip IP for semiconductor design, offering significant productivity gains.
news.summary: Arteris has transformed semiconductor design with their FlexGen technology, a smart Network-on-Chip IP that offers remarkable productivity enhancements and improved quality of results. This innovation will significantly impact the semiconductor industry, promising a surge in operational efficiency and effectiveness. The technology is projected to increase productivity by X% and improve quality by X%. This breakthrough underscores Arteris' commitment to advancing semiconductor design processes and advancing industry standards.

news.headline: Trump signed three actions.
Outrigger Energy II sells Williston Basin system to Hiland Partners Holdings LLC for $640 million.
news.summary: Outrigger Energy II LLC has recently finalized the sale of its midstream system in the Williston Basin of North Dakota to Hiland Partners Holdings LLC, a subsidiary of Kinder Morgan, Inc. The transaction was completed for a total of $640 million. The midstream system being sold includes a 270 million cubic feet per day cryogenic gas processing plant, about 104 miles of high-pressure, large diameter gathering pipelines, and 6,720 horsepower of field compression. This sale marks a significant move in the industry and showcases profitable opportunities in the North Dakota region.

news.headline: Match Group's CFO Steven Bailey will speak at the Morgan Stanley Conference on Mar 3, 2025, at 1pm Eastern time.
news.summary: On February 18, 2025, in Dallas, Match Group (NASDAQ: MTCH) shared news of Steven Bailey, the new Chief Financial Officer, set to take part in a fireside chat at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference on March 3 at 1:00 p.m. Eastern.

news.headline: RE/MAX National Housing Report: New listings up 53.7% in January with median sales price drop; inventory stable compared to last year.
news.summary: In February 18, 2025, it was reported that there has been a significant rise in new listings and inventory across 52 metro areas. The data showed a notable 53.7% increase in new listings in January compared to December. Interestingly, the number of new listings in January 2025 was almost equivalent to that of January 2024, with just a minor decline of 0.1%. This surge in new listings suggests a positive trend in the real estate market. Moreover, the inventory in January also witnessed growth, indicating a potential increase in housing options for buyers. However, despite these increments, there was a decrease in the median sales price from the previous month. The statistics reflect a dynamic market scenario with evolving trends in supply and demand.

news.headline: SSR Mining reports Q4 and full-year 2024 results, with production of 124,154 gold equivalent ounces.
news.summary: SSR Mining Inc. has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full-year ending on December 31, 2024, along with an update on the February 13, 2024 incident at the Çöpler mine. In the fourth quarter of 2024, the company reported production of 124,154 gold equivalent ounces. These figures are reported in U.S. dollars unless specified otherwise. The company's stocks are listed on the Nasdaq/TSX under SSRM and on the ASX under SSR.

news.headline: BrightSpire Capital, Inc. (NYSE: BRSP) announced Q4 2024 results: GAAP net loss of ($19.7M), Distributable Earnings of $13.7M, Adjusted Distributable Earnings of $23.7M.

news.headline: Cryptocurrency market slides; BTC at $94,100.04 (-1.8%), ETH $2,637.93 (-3.7%), XRP $2.51 (-5.5%), DOGE $0.2472 (-4.3%). Record BTC transactions, 140,769 traders liquidated, total $335.77M. Altcoin liquidity and market quality concerns. Robinhood expands crypto in Singapore.
news.summary: Cryptocurrency markets have seen a decline in prices despite positive factors like increased institutional investment and the proposal for Texas’ Strategic Bitcoin Reserve. Bitcoin is at $94,100.04 with a 1.8% loss, Ethereum at $2,637.93 down by 3.7%, Solana at $166.02 showing a 7.3% decrease, XRP at $2.51 down 5.5%, Dogecoin at $0.2472 down 4.3%, and Shiba Inu at $0.00001511, down 3.6%. There has been a notable increase in large transaction volumes by 14.5% and a 22.3% surge in daily active addresses. Transactions over $100,000 have jumped from 6,580 to 9,049 in a day, while exchange netflows have decreased by 127%. Coinglass data reveals 140,769 traders liquidated in the past day, totaling $335.77 million in liquidations. In related news, concerns have been raised over altcoin liquidity and market quality due to record-breaking token creation in January, and Robinhood is expanding its crypto offerings in Singapore.

news.headline: Kory Marchisotto appointed to Dutch Bros Board of Directors, expertise in Gen Z, Gen Alpha, noted beauty brands.
news.summary: A drive-thru beverage company, Dutch Bros Inc. (NYSE: BROS), based on the west coast, has appointed Kory Marchisotto to its Board of Directors as an independent director. Known for making a significant impact with each cup, Dutch Bros aims to connect with Gen Z and Gen Alpha customers. Marchisotto, with years of executive experience in renowned beauty brands, joins the board to lend her expertise in this aspect. The addition of Marchisotto to the board reflects Dutch Bros' focus on catering to younger generations.

The board will assess and decide on future dividends, as well as the record and payment dates, on a quarterly basis.

As part of the partnership, Goddess Maintenance Co. has committed to purchasing $4 million worth of b-silk products annually from Bolt.

news.summary: In Rutland, Vermont, on February 18, 2025, Casella Waste Systems, Inc. (Nasdaq: CWST), a company specializing in solid waste, recycling, and resource management services, disclosed its involvement in upcoming investor conferences. The management will be participating in these events.

news.headline: Rush Enterprises, Inc. reported $7.8B annual revenues, $304.2M net income, $2.0B 4th quarter revenues, and announced $0.18/share dividend.
news.summary: Rush Enterprises, Inc. reported annual revenues of $7.8 billion and net income of $304.2 million for the year ended December 31, 2024. Quarterly revenues were $2.0 billion with a net income of $74.8 million. Earnings per diluted share were $3.72 for the year and $0.91 for the fourth quarter. A cash dividend of $.18 per share of Class A and Class B common stock was declared. The company sold 38,615 new and used commercial vehicles in 2024. Rush Truck Leasing operates 57 franchises with over 10,100 trucks in its lease and rental fleet. The company repurchased $16.4 million of its common stock and paid $14.3 million in cash dividends during 2024. New U.S. and Canadian Class 8 retail truck sales totaled 275,184 units in 2024 with an overall commercial vehicle market forecasted to increase in 2025. Rush Enterprises emphasized strategic initiatives and network growth in the commercial vehicle industry while also focusing on financial performance and shareholder value. Rush Truck Centers serve as a premier commercial vehicle solutions provider, offering various services in addition to vehicle sales.

news.headline: Safe Bulkers, Inc. reports Q4 2024 net income of $19.4M, declares $0.05 dividend per common stock share; 46 vessels in fleet.
news.summary: Safe Bulkers, Inc. announced its unaudited financial results for Q4 and the twelve months ended December 31, 2024. Net revenues for Q4 2024 were $71.5 million, with a net income of $19.4 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $40.7 million for Q4 2024. The company declared a cash dividend of $0.05 per share, paid in March 2025. Safe Bulkers operated 45.90 vessels on average in Q4 2024, earning a Time Charter Equivalent rate of $16,521. The company had total debt of $536.6 million and 46 vessels in its fleet. The management team discussed the weaker charter market in Q4, decreased revenues, and increased operating expenses. The Board of Directors declared cash dividends for common and preferred shares. The company discussed ongoing investments in fleet renewal to comply with environmental regulations. The financial information was shared through a conference call and webcast. The impacts of the war in Ukraine and trade disruptions in the Middle East were also highlighted.

news.headline: Adeia Inc. achieved record revenue and cash flow in Q4, with $119.2 million in revenue, $107.5 million from operations, and 10 new deals. Paid down $50M of debt and repurchased $20M of stock. Plans for growth in 2025 include revenue outlook of $390M - $430M.
news.summary: Adeia Inc. reported record post-separation revenue and cash from operations in Q4, signing 10 deals totaling 32 for the year. Revenue for the quarter was $119.2 million, up from $86.1 million in Q3, with GAAP net income of $36.0 million and $107.5 million cash flow from operations. They paid down $50 million debt and repurchased $20 million of common stock. Full year 2024 revenue was $376.0 million, with GAAP net income of $64.6 million and cash flow from operations of $212.5 million. They paid down $114.2 million in debt for the year. Adeia aims for revenue of $390.0 to $430.0 million in 2025 with a strong focus on new customer acquisitions in growth markets. They also plan to maintain a high renewal rate in core markets. Adeia's adjusted EBITDA for 2025 outlook is $226.3 to $258.3 million. Non-GAAP financial measures reflect ongoing business performance. The company's stock repurchase plan has $180.0 million remaining as of December 31, 2024. Safe Harbor Statement and Forward-Looking Statements caution about risks and uncertainties involved. Adeia is a leading R&D and intellectual property licensing company in the media and semiconductor industries.

news.headline: US judge denies a request by State Attorneys General to temporarily bar Elon Musk's DOGE team from six government departments and OPM - Court Filing.

ORIC Pharmaceuticals reported 2024 financial results, with over 7 data readouts anticipated for its clinical programs. $256M in cash to fund into late 2026. Encouraging early data for ORIC-944 in combination with AR inhibitors in mCRPC and clinical collaboration agreement for ORIC-114 in NSCLC treatment. Multiple milestones set, such as potential registrational trials starting in 2H25 and early 2026. Expenses increased in R&D and G&A. Company focused on overcoming resistance in cancer with promising product candidates.
news.summary: ORIC Pharmaceuticals, a clinical stage oncology company, reported early safety and efficacy data for ORIC-944 in combination with androgen receptor inhibitors in mCRPC patients. They have also entered a collaboration with Johnson & Johnson to evaluate ORIC-114 in combination with amivantamab for NSCLC patients. The company expects seven data readouts over the next 18 months and plans for registrational trials starting in 2H25 and early 2026. They have a cash reserve of $256 million to fund operations into late 2026. ORIC-114 and ORIC-944 programs have shown promising results and the company anticipates several milestones in the coming years. Financially, their R&D expenses have increased, and they also secured $125 million in financing. ORIC focuses on overcoming resistance in cancer through precision medicines, including ORIC-114 and ORIC-944. Their leadership team has been expanded, and they are dedicated to developing innovative therapies for cancer patients.

news.headline: NeuroPace completes public offering, selling 7.5 million shares at $10 each, raising $74.8 million. Underwriters' option exercised.
news.summary: NeuroPace, Inc., based in Mountain View, California, disclosed on February 18, 2025, the finalization of its underwritten public offering. The company completed the sale of 7,475,000 shares of its common stock at $10.00 per share, which encompassed 975,000 shares exercised by the underwriters’ full purchase option. The entirety of shares were offered by NeuroPace, yielding an estimated $74.8 million in gross proceeds from the transaction, before factoring in underwriting discounts, commissions, and other expenses.

news.headline: MasterBrand Inc. (NYSE: MBC), largest NA cabinet manufacturer, reports Q4 and full year 2024 results, facing volume declines due to holiday season choppiness.
news.summary: Beachwood, Ohio-based MasterBrand, Inc. (NYSE: MBC) disclosed its financial outcomes for the fourth quarter and full year of 2024. The firm, the primary residential cabinet producer in North America, revealed that challenges in the end market escalated during the holiday period, causing unexpected drops in sales volume. This hindered the anticipated benefits of earlier price adjustments and constrained the company's ability to adjust operations effectively during the quarter. Dave Banyard, the spokesperson, addressed these issues.
